title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum fell to 3,468.50 USD/T on April 2, 2026, down 1.57% from the previous day, amid fears of a supply squeeze following President Trump's statements on the Iran war. Over the past month, prices have risen 5.83%, and are up 41.22% from last year. Forecasts indicate trading at 3517.79 USD/T by quarter-end and 3676.96 in 12 months.

title: Aluminium Price Today
summary: The current Aluminium futures price is 3,468.50, matching the previous close. Comex aluminium premiums beyond August have dipped due to bets on US tariff rollback. This reflects ongoing market adjustments in response to policy expectations.

title: World's Top Aluminium Smelter Cuts Output Due to Shipping Chaos
summary: The world's largest single-site aluminium smelter in Bahrain, Aluminium Bahrain (Alba), cut its output by about 20% due to shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. This shutdown affects 19% of Alba's 1.62 million tons annual capacity, representing roughly 2.2% of global aluminium production, to preserve raw material inventory. The disruption could tighten global supply chains and push aluminium prices higher.

title: China's Aluminum Output Hits Record While Steel Sinks
summary: China's aluminum production reached a record 45.02 million tons in 2025, up 2.4% from the previous year and nearing capacity limits, with December hitting 3.87 million tons. This contrasts with steel output declining 4.4% to 961 million tons, the lowest since 2019. The data underscores divergent trends for these key metals.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices fell to 3,103 CNY/T on April 3, 2026, down 0.06% from the previous day, amid demand concerns and policy uncertainty. Steel rebar futures extended losses to a three-week low due to reports of potential revisions to US tariffs on steel and aluminum products. Over the past month, prices have risen 0.62%.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel rose to 1,075 USD/T on April 2, 2026, up 0.47% from the previous day, marking the highest since January 2024. Over the past month, prices increased 5.08%, and year-over-year by 17.74%. Recent updates show continued gains in hot-rolled coil steel.

title: US considers revising Section 232 tariffs with lower rates on steel derivatives
summary: The US is considering revisions to Section 232 tariffs, potentially lowering rates on steel derivatives while keeping others near 50%. This follows reports impacting steel rebar futures and market sentiment. The news contributes to ongoing pressure on steel prices.

title: Iran’s Mobarakeh Steel confirms complete shutdown after second US-Israeli strike
summary: Iran's Mobarakeh Steel has shut down completely following a second US-Israeli strike, disrupting supply chains. This event aligns with mentions of Mideast disruptions affecting steel prices. Global market turbulence is expected from such geopolitical events.

title: MOC: Average high-speed wire rod price in China down 0.3% in Mar 23-29, 2026
summary: China's average high-speed wire rod price fell 0.3% during March 23-29, 2026, reflecting softening in certain steel segments. This trend contributes to broader concerns over Chinese steel demand. Prices remain under pressure amid various market factors.

title: Smelting Barriers: The Innovators Forging a New Future for Iron and Steel
summary: The article highlights 2026 as a landmark year for the steel industry, with Stegra set to produce and sell the first commercial volumes of near-zero emissions steel in Northern Sweden using green hydrogen and electrified processes. It discusses challenges like access to clean power and hydrogen, and innovations including novel production methods that could account for 30% of global emissions reductions by 2050. Third Derivative's initiatives support startups in decarbonizing steel through electrification, feedstock innovations, and disruptive technologies.

title: Construction of the direct reduced iron tower at Stegra in Boden, Sweden
summary: Stegra is building the world’s first plant for large-scale production of green hydrogen, green iron, and green steel in Boden, Sweden. The process replaces coal with green hydrogen to directly reduce iron ore, then combines green iron with recycled scrap in an electrified mill to produce near-zero-emission steel. This innovation is featured in the 2026 April issue of AIST publications.

title: Live Copper Prices Today
summary: Copper prices declined by 0.58%, trading at $5.64/lb globally and ¥85,624/Ton in China. The pullback is driven by surging global inventories in LME and SHFE warehouses, easing supply squeeze dynamics. Slower Chinese grid and property momentum, along with rising energy costs from Middle East tensions and US Treasury yields, are suppressing demand and investor appetite.

title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Copper rose to 5.55 USD/Lbs on March 31, 2026, up 1.30% from the previous day, though down 5.89% over the past month amid Middle East disruptions and surging energy prices. Prices held near $5.5 per pound but faced pressure from inflation concerns and slowing global industrial activity. Hopes for US-Iran diplomatic resolution supported a potential weekly gain.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart follows recent operational challenges at the site.

title: No sign yet of China’s plan to cut copper output in major smelters’ results
summary: Major smelters are planning to raise or maintain output in 2026, despite a public commitment by the state-linked industry association to cut production by over 10%. This indicates no immediate implementation of China's copper output reduction plan. The article highlights ongoing production strategies amid market pressures.

title: Copper: Chile output slump and mine cuts
summary: Chile's copper output is experiencing a slump due to mine cuts and operational challenges. This contributes to global supply constraints affecting copper production. The report analyzes the impact on market dynamics.

title: Copper-Aluminum Divergence Unveils The Complex Iran War Impact
summary: The divergence between copper and aluminum prices reveals complicated effects from the Iran war on metal markets. Copper production and supply chains face disruptions tied to geopolitical tensions. This influences capacity and output forecasts.

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto has produced the first copper cathode using its proprietary Nuton bioleaching technology at the Johnson Camp mine, achieving the lowest mine-to-metal carbon footprint in the U.S. at 0.82 kg CO₂-e per kg copper. The technology, developed over 30 years, enables faster and cleaner production, with 100% renewable energy matching via certificates. It targets 30,000 tonnes of refined copper over four years and supports the U.S. domestic supply chain.

title: Copper Mining in the US: Efficiency & Sustainability
summary: Minetek enhances U.S. copper mining efficiency and sustainability through ventilation, water management, and sound solutions, amid projected demand doubling by 2035. Innovations like Zero Liquid Discharge and GISTM-aligned practices support projects such as Resolution Copper, expected to supply 25% of future U.S. output. These technologies achieve up to 50% improvements in evaporation efficiency, energy savings, and noise suppression while cutting costs by 60%.

title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap
summary: Amid a projected 10 Mt copper supply shortfall by 2035 due to rising demand from renewables, EVs, and AI data centers, innovations target primary sulfide ores, energy-efficient mining, and reduced environmental impacts. Developments include novel grinding technologies to improve extraction efficiency. These efforts address the need for $250 billion in capital to expand production from 26 Mt in 2024 to 35 Mt by 2035.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel futures are trading around $17,200, down from March highs near $17,770, amid structural market tightness and cautious investor sentiment. Supply constraints persist due to Indonesia's proposed nickel export tax and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. Nickel traded flat at 17,100 USD/T on April 3, 2026, with a 2.56% monthly decline but 16.80% yearly gain.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Nickel prices remained flat at $17,081.3/ton globally and ¥117,559/ton in China. A firm price floor is supported by Indonesia's reduced ore production quotas, easing oversupply concerns, along with rising coal and sulphur input costs and recovering Chinese EV and stainless steel demand. Upside is capped by Indonesian royalty rate increases at higher price levels, keeping prices range-bound.

title: Nickel Industries showcases sustainability in quarterly update
summary: Nickel Industries Limited reported stable operational performance in its Quarterly Activities Report, highlighting strong safety metrics with a 12-month Company-wide Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate (LTIFR) of 0.00 and no lost-time injuries during the period. Over the past 12 months, the company achieved 17.7 million safe man-hours while maintaining a low Total Recordable Injury Frequency Rate (TRIFR) of 0.68. The update emphasizes the company's commitment to sustainability in nickel operations.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia's PT Weda Bay Nickel, operating the world's biggest nickel mine, has been notified to cut ore production quota to 12 million tons this year from 42 million tons in 2025 to boost global nickel prices. The company intends to apply for a revision amid rising nickel prices following the output cut announcement. Nickel is essential for stainless steel and electric-vehicle batteries.
